growth_records.medium: CSM3 (Cedars Standard Medium 3) with acetate + H2 (mixotrophic)
growth_records.cite: Lina J. Bird et al. Serpentinimonas gen. nov., Serpentinimonas raichei sp. nov., Serpentinimonas barnesii sp. nov. and Serpentinimonas maccroryi sp. nov., hyperalkaliphilic and facultative autotrophic bacteria isolated from terrestrial serpentinizing springs. International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1099/ijsem.0.004945. [PMC8513617]
growth_records.snippet: During mixotrophic growth on hydrogen and acetate, the minimum doubling time was 10 h for strain A1T, 12.5 h for strain H1T and 8.5 h for strain B1T, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Reported minimum doubling time td=8.5 h (fastest of the three strains). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/8.5 = 0.0815 /h. Mixotrophic H2 + 3 mM acetate. 30 C, pH 11 optimum (B1 grew even at pH 12 in chemostat), microaerophilic (1-4% O2). Fatty-acid analysis used acetate-grown chemostat cultures but no dilution/uptake rate given. No uptake/secretion rates measured.
